dataset: NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey)
case_id: dynamically selected patient from NHANES cohort

pi_items.source: Mayo Clinic, Drugs.com, MedlinePlus, FDA label confirmation - Lisinopril is an FDA-approved ACE inhibitor available in 10mg tablet form
Standard medical allergy documentation practice - medication allergies are recorded using the drug name

pi_items.verified_value: Dry cough (also reported as persistent, non-productive, tickly cough)
pi_items.source: FDA adverse reaction data (Zestril label), Mayo Clinic, Drugs.com, GoodRx, PubMed (NIH), NHS - Dry cough is a well-established, class-effect adverse reaction to ACE inhibitors including Lisinopril, occurring in 4-35% of patients (incidence varies by study: 1-6% to 10-15%)
Clinical practice guidelines and FDA adverse reaction documentation - ACE inhibitor-induced cough is recognized as a significant adverse reaction warranting medication discontinuation

pi_items.verified_value: NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey) - real de-identified CDC data
pi_items.source: setup_librehealth.sh downloads libreehr_nhanes.sql.gz from LibreHealthIO/lh-ehr GitHub (https://github.com/LibreHealthIO/lh-ehr), containing 9,375 real NHANES patients from 2011-2012 NHANES period

pi_items.verified_value: LibreHealth EHR (open-source electronic health record system)
pi_items.source: https://github.com/LibreHealthIO/lh-ehr - task setup scripts and environment configuration files confirm use of LibreHealth EHR via Docker

setup_task.sh explicitly creates Lisinopril medication record: 'INSERT INTO lists (pid, type, date, title, begdate, activity, user) VALUES (${TARGET_PID}, 'medication', NOW(), 'Lisinopril 10mg', '${START_DATE}', 1, 'admin')'

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic - to be created by agent during task execution
pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py indicate the agent must create the allergy record as part of the task completion

privileged_info_summary: Lisinopril 10mg is a real FDA-approved ACE inhibitor blood pressure medication. Dry cough is a well-documented, class-effect adverse reaction to ACE inhibitors, reported in the FDA label and occurring in 4-35% of treated patients. Discontinuation due to this adverse reaction is standard clinical practice. The task uses LibreHealth EHR with real NHANES patient data (9,375 de-identified US patients from CDC's National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey), though the Lisinopril medication record is synthetically injected for task purposes. The clinical scenario (adverse drug reaction management) is realistic and medically sound.
